seo.og_title: WorkOS — Pricing, Developer Experience & Best Use Cases
seo.og_description: WorkOS for Next.js App Router: Advanced learning curve, Custom pricing (enterprise), 45 minutes setup. Enterprise SSO/SCIM for B2B SaaS  –  no free tier, sales call required, pair with consumer auth

hero.headline: WorkOS: Complete Review for Next.js Developers
hero.subheadline: A thorough breakdown of WorkOS's pricing, developer experience, and fit for Next.js App Router projects.

intro_paragraphs: WorkOS targets enterprise sso/scim for b2b saas  –  no free tier, sales call required, pair with consumer auth. With a Next.js integration score of 5/10 and a advanced learning curve, it fits a specific niche in the auth space. The real question: does that niche match your project?
who_should_use_paragraphs: Who is WorkOS actually for? Teams that need a reliable solution for enterprise-grade sso with saml/oidc support out of the box and scim provisioning for enterprise directory integration. The advanced learning curve and Custom pricing (enterprise) pricing tell you whether this is a match.
pricing_context: WorkOS does not publish pricing and requires a sales call - a red flag for early-stage startups. Enterprise SSO typically costs $5,000-25,000+/year as an add-on with platforms like Auth0. WorkOS is likely similar. Additionally, WorkOS only solves enterprise auth (SSO, SCIM); you still need a separate solution for consumer auth (email/password, social login). The total cost for auth could easily exceed $1,000/mo. For most Next.js B2B SaaS startups, Auth0's comprehensive platform (consumer + SSO) at $3,000/mo may actually be cheaper than running WorkOS + Clerk. Evaluate WorkOS only when you have enterprise customers actively requesting SSO.

faq.question: Is WorkOS good for Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: WorkOS has a Next.js integration score of 5/10. WorkOS takes a different approach than traditional auth platforms - it focuses specifically on enterprise features like SSO, SCIM, and directory sync. WorkOS is a capable option for App Router projects.

faq.question: How much does WorkOS cost?
faq.answer: WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with No free tier (enterprise sales). WorkOS does not publish pricing and requires a sales call - a red flag for early-stage startups. Factor in your expected MAU when evaluating total cost.

faq.question: How long does it take to set up WorkOS with Next.js?
faq.answer: Setup time is approximately 45 minutes with a advanced learning curve. The Next.
